title: From The Whole To A Part
study: A Study In The Life Of John The
scriptureForWeek: VARIOUS PASSAGES IN THE GOSPELS Make sure you pay attention to the specific verses chosen for this week. They are designed to highlight John the Baptist handing his attention and ministry over to Jesus.
weekTheme: Boys are Satisfied; Men want More God will give you a hunger for more This is now week 7 of the third shift in the Primal Path, from the Whole to a Part. The big idea here is to learn who you are, what role God has for you, and how to play your part in the story of God. Most guys your age take decades to learn this lesson, so you are doing really, really well so far. Keep focusing and keep showing up! This week is a MAJOR week in the shift, because it’s at this point that John the Baptist begins to fade out, and Jesus takes center stage. John knows he is not the star of the story, just the man God used to get people ready. He joyfully gives the attention to Jesus because he knew that in every way possible, Jesus was better than him and it belonged to him. But this doesn’t mean it’s easy. It’s hard to let go of the center. It’s hard to honor Jesus when you are worried about being forgotten, overlooked, or left out. So this week we are going to see why we can trust Jesus and how to find joy and relief in letting him be the center of the story. This kind of understanding will give you a massive advantage in your generation, which is filled with insecure, striving, and competing men who don’t know how disappointed they will be with pride and selfishness. Find your place in the story of God. Take your place in the story of God. Remember the story is about Jesus. Here is a quick little reminder of why if you have 2 minutes: https://vimeo.com/23642755

days.title: Better Relationship
days.scripture: MATTHEW 3:13-17 Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John. But John tried to deter him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?” Jesus replied, “Let it be so now; it is proper for us to do this to fulfill all righteousness.” Then John consented. As soon as Jesus was baptized, he went up out of the water. At that moment heaven was opened, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him. And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.”
days.reflection: What is a relationship with God actually supposed to be like? You have probably heard people around you talking about a “personal relationship with God” for most of your life. But what does that really mean? It can be hard to communicate with an invisible being. It can be hard to read the Bible when it was written so long ago and is filled with stuff that can seem weird today. The Bible can be hard to understand. What exactly is this personal relationship with God? In today’s reading, you see one of the clearest pictures in the whole Bible of the core elements of our relationship with God. It is summarized in 13 words. These 13 words are the most important thing a man will ever hear. And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.” Acceptance. This is my son. Affection. Whom I love. Affirmation. With him I am well pleased. These are the key elements of a relationship with God. ACCEPTANCE Every guy your age wants to fit in. We all want to know that we are loved for who we really are. We don’t want to have to pretend to be someone else so others like us. That is exhausting. We want to be seen and loved as we are. Jesus came to save us and bring us into a relationship with our heavenly father. Because of Jesus’ work, we are accepted by God just like he is. God sees you, loves you, and knows you. AFFECTION Every guy your age wants to be liked. To be celebrated, not just tolerated. You have to realize that God doesn’t just put up with you because he has to; he delights in you because he wants to. His heart toward you is kind, and he understands your struggles. That’s why 1 John 3:1 says, “See what great love the Father has lavished on us, that we should be called children of God! And that is what we are!” You are lavished in love. AFFIRMATION Every guy your age aches for encouragement. People can be so critical today and constantly beat us down. But the voice of God spoken over us says this: “I am pleased with you.” So many walk around with a sense of failure and disappointment. But salvation through Jesus brings us into God's family. God doesn’t just love you; he likes you. He is for you and not against you. Nothing can separate you from his love. It's such a joy to know that the acceptance, affection, and affirmation you long for are freely given to you through Jesus. You don’t have to pretend, perform and puff yourself up. God loved you when you were a sinner; he delights in you as his son. God's deepest desire for you is that you experience and enjoy his love. This is the wonder and heart of the gospel. You’ve been adopted as his son, you’ve been lavished with his love, and you’ve inherited the riches of his grace. No one has the power to shame or cancel you before our God. John the Baptist wanted the world to know the power of Jesus’ salvation. I hope you have come to know it for yourself. I hope you get a vision of becoming like John and telling other guys your age the 13 words that they long to hear. These are the words that all men long to hear.
questions: What is one of the kindest things anyone has said to you? Why was this so helpful?
What is one of the most hurtful things someone has said to you? Why was this so painful for you?
Ask your Dad about a defining moment he first realized that God didn’t just tolerate him, but really loved him. What happened, and what impact did it make in his life?

thoughts.verse: For his unfailing love toward those who fear him is as great as the height of the heavens above the earth. He has removed our sins as far from us as the east is from the west. The LORD is like a father to his children, tender and compassionate to those who fear him. For he knows how weak we are; he remembers we are only dust.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to show you his love in a fresh way. Ask him to increase the volume of his voice in your life.

days.title: Better Security
days.scripture: JOHN 1:35-42 The following day John was again standing with two of his disciples. As Jesus walked by, John looked at him and declared, “Look! There is the Lamb of God!” When John’s two disciples heard this, they followed Jesus. Jesus looked around and saw them following. “What do you want?” he asked them. They replied, “Rabbi” (which means “Teacher”), “where are you staying?” “Come and see,” he said. It was about four o’clock in the afternoon when they went with him to the place where he was staying, and they remained with him the rest of the day. Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of these men who heard what John said and then followed Jesus. Andrew went to find his brother, Simon, and told him, “We have found the Messiah” (which means “Christ”). Then Andrew brought Simon to meet Jesus. Looking intently at Simon, Jesus said, “Your name is Simon, son of John—but you will be called Cephas” (which means “Peter”).
days.reflection: There is a concept in psychology called imposter syndrome. This is when someone feels like they are pretending to be something they are not. They may doubt that they have what it takes even if they are projecting confidence. They feel inadequate on the inside, even if other people can’t tell. Guys your age talk about the same thing, but they have a simpler phrase to describe this. Faking it. So many guys today are faking it. They pretend to be strong, or tough, or act like things don’t hurt them, but on the inside, they are often really insecure. They talk a good game but often struggle with their own sense of worth, value, and place in the world. John the Baptist was a deeply secure man, with no imposter syndrome. He watched as his disciples walk away from him and went to follow Jesus, and he did this with joy. Imagine all the time, effort, love, conversation, and training John poured into these guys, only to have them walk away. Yet John doesn’t seem to mind. He had a deep inner security that showed itself in moments like this. One of the reasons John the Baptist wanted people to see Jesus and follow him was because he knew that Jesus had the ability to break the imposter syndrome in a man's life and give him a true sense of security. John had experienced this himself, and he got joy knowing others would find it in Jesus. How does Jesus do this? 1- Jesus bases our relationship with him on his love, not ours. We are held by the strength of his love, not our performance or feelings. That’s why in John 15 he tells us to “remain in his love,” not our feelings. It's about how he feels about you more than how you feel about him. We can rest in that. 2- Jesus is full of mercy. He is willing to forgive us when we fail and restore us when we screw up. So we are freed from thinking we have to be perfect, or that God will be mad at us when we struggle. You don’t have to deal with shame that so many guys your age deal with because of Jesus’ compassionate love. He cares, understands, and shows mercy. 3- Jesus has a plan for us, and we don’t have to strive to prove ourselves. God has stuff for you, and you just need to walk with him, discern what it is, and live it out. You don’t have to be the coolest, smartest, most successful person. You can just live your calling. Knowing you are doing his will frees you from having to do everyone else’s. If you are going to grow into a man who can play his role in the story of God with deep security and resist faking it to fit in, you are going to need an internal source of love. It all starts by rooting yourself in him. Seeing God's love, affection, and affirmation for you. Get your roots down deep in that, and you will have a source of security to draw from. You can relax; you don’t need to fake it anymore. Just be who God has called you to be.
questions: Do you see any of your friends wrestling with “imposter syndrome” or “faking it”? How do you sense that’s happening?
On a scale of 1-10 (1 being the lowest, 10 highest), how confident and secure do you feel right now? Why? Is it the same in all the areas of your life?
Ask your Dad if he has ever wrestled with imposter syndrome or insecurity. Ask him to tell you about it. How did he learn to get a source of secure identity and overcome it?

thoughts.verse: I have loved you even as the Father has loved me. Remain in my love.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a fresh sense of his love and to make your heart secure and steadfast in that.

days.title: Better Identity
days.scripture: JOHN 1:35-42 The following day John was again standing with two of his disciples. As Jesus walked by, John looked at him and declared, “Look! There is the Lamb of God!” When John’s two disciples heard this, they followed Jesus. Jesus looked around and saw them following. “What do you want?” he asked them. They replied, “Rabbi” (which means “Teacher”), “where are you staying?” “Come and see,” he said. It was about four o’clock in the afternoon when they went with him to the place where he was staying, and they remained with him the rest of the day. Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of these men who heard what John said and then followed Jesus. Andrew went to find his brother, Simon, and told him, “We have found the Messiah” (which means “Christ”). Then Andrew brought Simon to meet Jesus. Looking intently at Simon, Jesus said, “Your name is Simon, son of John-but you will be called Cephas” (which means “Peter”).
days.reflection: One of the most important things you will learn growing up is this: You get your sense of worth based on the person whose opinion you value the most. Read that again slowly, and let that sink in. Whose opinion do you value the most? Is it your parents? Is it a girl you like? Is it a teacher at school? Is it a couple of your friends? It’s important to be aware of this because you don’t want to grant too much power to someone who doesn’t deserve it. John the Baptist got his sense of worth from Jesus. And today, Peter is going to learn how to get his sense of worth from Jesus too. Peter ends up being introduced to Jesus when some of his friends leave John and follow Jesus. Then something really intense happens. Look at this verse again. Looking intently at Simon, Jesus said, “Your name is Simon, son of John—but you will be called Cephas” (which means “Peter”). Can you imagine meeting Jesus and he starts giving you a stare-down? Looking at you really intently, and then giving you a new name? Talk about a weird introduction. But Jesus is doing something powerful here. Something he wants to do in your life. He is calling Peter who he sees him to be, not just who others see him as. Jesus is going to change his name to Peter, which means stone, or rock. He is telling Peter that he sees something in him that Peter doesn’t even see in himself. When you read the gospels, Peter is a bit of a wild man. He talks more than all the other apostles put together. He is full of passion but full of mistakes. He promises not to deny Jesus, but does it 3 times in one night. YET, Jesus restores him and Peter goes on to become a founder of Jesus’ church. If Peter relied on his own opinion, he would probably have named himself “failure” or “flakey,” certainly not “the rock!” However, Jesus loved Peter, believed in him and restored him, He defined him by his call and not his failure. Jesus is the only one who can see who we really are and define you according to your purpose and not your failures. Life is gonna throw a lot of hard things at you. Conflict and fights, rejection and betrayal, discouragement and evil. You are going to need a solid center not to be shaken. You need to see yourself as Jesus does, not how you do, or the world does. You are who he says you are, period.
questions: Whose opinions do guys your age tend to think are the most valuable? Older guys, friends, girls? How do you see this show up in how they act?
Can you take a moment to think about 2 or 3 people whose opinions you really value? How do they shape how you see yourself?
Ask your Dad to share how he learned to let God's vision of him define his life, rather than just the people around him. Ask him to share a practical example of how he wrestled through this.

thoughts.verse: Obviously, I’m not trying to win the approval of people, but of God. If pleasing people were my goal, I would not be Christ’s servant.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to strengthen your identity in him. Ask him for a vision of how he sees you to grip your heart.

days.title: Better Leaders
days.scripture: JOHN 1:32-34 Then John testified, “I saw the Holy Spirit descending like a dove from heaven and resting upon him. I didn’t know he was the one, but when God sent me to baptize with water, he told me, ‘The one on whom you see the Spirit descend and rest is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.’ I saw this happen to Jesus, so I testify that he is the Chosen One of God.”
days.reflection: You are living at a time of history when leadership is in crisis. You may not think about leadership that much at your age, but it’s really important. Most of what is wrong in our world come down to a failure of leadership. In Jesus’ day, most of the leaders were corrupt. People longed for change. That’s why John the Baptist was so courageous. He called for a new leader; he called for Jesus. John critiqued the oppression of Rome, the hypocrisy of the Jewish leaders, and the failure of the Pharisees. He wanted them all to realize that their time was coming to an end, and the leader the world longed for was on the way. John knew who Jesus was, what he could do, and the kind of kingdom he would bring into the world. Everyone today has a vision of how to make the world a better place. Yet so often these human leaders disappoint us. Leaders promise one thing but deliver another. They say one thing and do another. Yet Jesus is the leader that doesn’t let us down. Jesus used his power to serve and not oppress. Jesus stuck up for those who were being bullied by others. Jesus gave hope to those who felt like failures. Jesus forgave his enemies, rather than destroyed them. Jesus was loyal to his followers, even when they betrayed him. Jesus rose from the dead, proving he has the power to build an eternal kingdom. Your generation needs leadership. So many guys your age are disillusioned with the hype of churches, the hypocrisy of leaders, and the failures of celebrities. You need to determine in your heart that you are going to make Jesus the leader of your life and that you will point others to his leadership. When Jesus shows up, love shows up. When Jesus shows up, hope shows up. When Jesus shows up, justice shows up. John pointed to Jesus as the leader the world needed. Your generation is longing for a leader like Jesus. God has given you the role of pointing people to Christ.
questions: Can you name someone you know who you think has done a good job leading something? Why? What impresses you about them?
Do you consider yourself a leader? Why or why not?
If you could list 5 traits of what a person needs to be a good leader, what would they be?

thoughts.verse: “The Spirit of the LORD is upon me, for he has anointed me to bring Good News to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim that captives will be released, that the blind will see, that the oppressed will be set free, and that the time of the LORD’s favor has come.”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you from it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to surrender yourself to the leadership of Jesus. Ask God to show you how relevant and revolutionary it is in the world today.

days.title: A Vision Of Something Better
days.scripture: JOHN 12:30-32 Then Jesus told them, “The voice was for your benefit, not mine. The time for judging this world has come, when Satan, the ruler of this world, will be cast out. And when I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to myself.” He said this to indicate how he was going to die.
days.reflection: This week you have looked at a pivotal moment in the life of John the Baptist. It’s a moment that shows the kind of men we need in the world today. John is giving the attention and influence that he has gained over to Jesus. He has lived for the purpose that God gave him, being a forerunner to prepare the way of Jesus, and now he is enjoying seeing the ministry of Jesus take off. John played a role in Jesus story, but he wasn’t the point of the story. He got this balance just right. Have you noticed that when sports stars win they often point to the sky or thank God in their post-game interviews? Sometimes actors and musicians do the same thing when they win awards. Why? At times this can seem cheesy, or even weird because it can seem hard to see how Jesus has much to do with writing a song, hitting a home run, or winning a game. But these folks have a deep awareness of how much they have been blessed by God, and how much they depend on him for their success. Because you are growing up in a world where everyone craves attention, you will have to determine who will get the credit in your life as you grow up. The more you grow and accomplish, people will want to understand your motivation for what you do. They will want to know the source of your strength. The earlier you settle this in your heart, the better your life will be as a man. You need to determine to be like John the Baptist. To point people to Jesus with the influence and platform you have. The men we need in the world today are men who lift Jesus up. The men we need are those who acknowledge God's goodness and kindness in their lives and are not filled with arrogance and pride. The men we need are those who do their best but realize everything is founded on God's kindness and grace. As you grow up, point people to Jesus. Resist the narcissistic tendencies alive and well in your generation to center yourself. Be confident in your calling, but give the attention to Jesus. Jesus said that if he is lifted up, he will draw people to himself. John the Baptist lifted Jesus up, and we are still being drawn to him today, thousands of years and thousands of miles away from when this happened. You never know the ripple effect your faith can have on those around you. When you get attention, give away the attention. This is the heart of godly men.
questions: Who are the most famous Christians you can think of? In what ways do they give attention or their platform to Jesus? How do people respond to this?
What do you think it means to lift Jesus up for a guy your age in everyday life, without being weird?
Ask your dad some of the ways he seeks to give the attention and glory to Jesus in his everyday life. How did he learn to do that as he was growing in his faith?

thoughts.verse: Not to us, O LORD, not to us, but to your name goes all the glory for your unfailing love and faithfulness.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a heart for Jesus. That you would know him at a deeper level and seek to please him in all you do.

extras: WEEK 7 MANSCHOOL TRICK SHOT MANSCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: TRICK SHOT Everyone has been amazed at what the guys from Dude Perfect have been able to pull off, but you can have a ton of fun trying something at your home. Here is some inspiration: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GsLopdvhFpk Try and start simple, and work your way up. Remember to celebrate when you finally make the shot. Try recording it too and share it with the family. OPTION 2. WATCH Chariots of Fire An absolute classic. Tells the story of ambition, fame, and what it means to honor God no matter what it costs. https://youtu.be/uPe27x0_W2M Also, the most fascinating thing is what happens after Eric Liddell wins the gold. You can read about it in the book For The Glory, which will be one of the required readings for the Primal Path summer program next summer. OPTION 3. DINNER AND DISCUSSION Take your son out to one of his favorite restaurants or for desserts and talk through what it means to struggle with not being the center of attention. There is a fine balance between being ignored and voluntarily
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
